channel.title: Frontion News Headlines
channel.description: Geopolitical news headlines curated by Frontion

item.title: 🇮🇱🇱🇧 Israel strikes Lebanon killing 12 ahead of US-brokered talks
item.description: Israeli airstrikes hit vehicles in Lebanon, killing 12 people one day before planned US-brokered negotiations between Lebanon and Israel in Washington.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇮🇷 US Senate blocks Iran war powers challenge as conflict continues
item.description: Senate Republicans blocked a Democratic-led effort to end the Iran war until Congress authorizes it, while support for congressional oversight grows.

item.title: 🇹🇼🇨🇳 Taiwan stages live-fire drill opposite mainland as Trump visits Beijing
item.description: Taiwan conducted live-fire military exercises on an island facing mainland China, highlighting anxiety about being used as a bargaining chip during the Trump-Xi summit.

item.title: 🇬🇧🛢️ UK Energy Secretary vows permanent North Sea shutdown amid Iran war supply crunch
item.description: Ed Miliband pledged to permanently shut down North Sea oil and gas production, though timing draws controversy as the Iran war disrupts Middle East energy supplies.

item.title: 🇪🇺🇨🇳 European firms in China rethink supply chains as Iran war drives costs higher
item.description: A new survey shows European companies operating in China are adjusting sourcing and production plans due to supply disruptions and rising costs from Middle East instability.

item.title: 💰 Fed officials signal rate hikes may be needed to curb inflation
item.description: Boston Fed President Collins and Minneapolis Fed President Kashkari both indicated interest rates may need to rise if inflation pressures persist, with the Iran war worsening price pressures.

item.title: 🇳🇬 Nigeria urges global finance overhaul as debt costs consume half of revenue
item.description: President Tinubu called for reforming the global financial system, saying Nigeria will spend .6 billion on debt service in 2026 - nearly half of projected government revenue.

item.title: 🇬🇧 Bank of England to hold rates at 3.75% as Iran war fuels inflation
item.description: The BoE will likely keep rates steady this year, though a growing minority of economists expect a hike as energy price surges from the Iran conflict drive up inflation forecasts.

item.title: 🇮🇱🇦🇪 Netanyahu Held Secret Meeting With UAE President During Iran War
item.description: Israeli PM Benjamin Netanyahu visited the UAE and met with the Emirati president during the Iran war, marking their first confirmed meeting and a "historic breakthrough" in relations.

item.title: 🇭🇺 Hungary Summons Russian Ambassador Over Western Ukraine Drone Strikes
item.description: Hungary summoned Russia's ambassador after drone attacks on western Ukraine near its border, highlighting NATO concerns as Russian strikes continue despite ongoing peace talks.

item.title: 🇸🇦🇮🇷 Saudi Arabia Launched Covert Attacks on Iran During Regional War
item.description: Saudi Arabia carried out previously unreported military strikes on Iranian soil and targeted Tehran-backed militias in Iraq, marking the first known direct Saudi military action against Iran.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇺🇦 US House Forces Vote on Ukraine Aid, Defying GOP Leadership
item.description: A discharge petition reached 218 signatures to force a House floor vote on Ukraine security aid and new Russia sanctions, bypassing Speaker Mike Johnson with bipartisan support.

item.title: 🇺🇸💰 Kevin Warsh Set for Senate Confirmation as Fed Chair Amid Inflation Surge
item.description: The US Senate is expected to confirm Kevin Warsh as Federal Reserve chair as inflation hits a three-year high, with markets watching for potential rate hikes.

item.title: 🇮🇷 Iran releases prominent human rights lawyer Nasrin Sotoudeh on bail
item.description: Iran has freed prominent human rights lawyer Nasrin Sotoudeh on bail after her arrest in early April during a crackdown. Sotoudeh is a leading Iranian rights defender who has been imprisoned multiple times for her activism.

item.title: 🇸🇦 Gulf states arrest over 100 Shiites on treason charges amid Iran war
item.description: Gulf nations have arrested more than 100 Shiites and accused them of treason as the Iran-Israel war continues, according to a New York Times report. The arrests highlight regional sectarian tensions amplified by the conflict.

item.title: 🇩🇪 Germany calls for probe into alleged Israeli abuse of Palestinian detainees
item.description: Germany has urged an investigation into allegations of torture and sexual abuse against Palestinian detainees in Israeli custody. The call adds international pressure over treatment of prisoners amid the broader regional conflict.

item.title: 🇯🇵 Japan fails to secure Trump stop in Tokyo before Beijing summit
item.description: Japanese government plans to invite President Trump to Japan ahead of his China visit fell through. The diplomatic setback comes as Trump arrives in Beijing for a high-stakes summit with Xi Jinping.

item.title: ⚔️ Anduril raises $5 billion to build autonomous warship operations
item.description: Defense tech company Anduril has secured $5 billion in financing to build autonomous warship operations in Seattle. The major investment signals growing private sector involvement in defense manufacturing.

item.title: 🇺🇦 Russia Launches Major Drone Barrage on Ukraine Despite Peace Talks
item.description: Russia fired approximately 800 drones at Ukraine in a prolonged barrage lasting hours, hitting Kyiv and Lviv despite recent Moscow talk of possible peace negotiations.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 Trump Arrives in Beijing for High-Stakes Xi Summit on Iran, Trade, Taiwan
item.description: President Trump landed in Beijing with top CEOs including Nvidia Jensen Huang and Elon Musk, seeking Chinese help on the Iran war while navigating tensions over tariffs, Taiwan, and AI technology.

item.title: 🇵🇭 Gunshots Erupt at Philippine Senate During ICC Arrest Standoff
item.description: Gunshots broke out at the Philippine Senate as security forces attempted to arrest Senator Ronald dela Rosa, wanted by the International Criminal Court for crimes against humanity related to the drug war.

item.title: 🇸🇪 Sweden Announces $1.9 Billion Energy Relief Package Amid Middle East War
item.description: Sweden will temporarily reduce fuel taxes and introduce measures to shield households and businesses from soaring energy costs caused by the Iran war disruption.

item.title: 🏛️ House Reaches Threshold to Force Vote on $1.3 Billion Ukraine Aid
item.description: A discharge petition reached 218 signatures to force a House vote on $1.3 billion in Ukraine aid, marking a significant procedural win for supporters.

item.title: 🇫🇷 France probes Israeli firm BlackCore over alleged election interference
item.description: French authorities are investigating whether an obscure Israeli company carried out a foreign interference campaign targeting hard-left France Unbowed candidates ahead of March municipal elections, including deceptive websites and disparaging digital ads.

item.title: 🇺🇸🇨🇳 US and China launch unprecedented joint operation against Dubai scam centres
item.description: The FBI and Chinese Ministry of Public Security collaborated with Dubai Police to dismantle nine fraud centres and arrest 276 suspects, in a rare show of cooperation ahead of Trump's China visit.
